extraction.one_line_summary: TEUNS Dylan won after surviving 24% gravel finish at La Planche des Belles Filles, while THOMAS Geraint attacked final km to distance all GC rivals.
tactical_patterns: Ineos reduced to single domestique Kwiatkowski before finale, revealing lack of depth compared to previous Tours dominance.
CICCONE Giulio collected 8 bonus seconds on penultimate climb Col des Chevres, tactical innovation that delivered yellow by 6 seconds.
THOMAS Geraint's attack came after Alaphilippe initiated move in final km, exploiting moment leader was already anaerobic on 24% gravel.
Movistar burned VALVERDE Alejandro early on front work, exposing team's confused hierarchy when unable to support Landa's later counter.

rider_signals.THOMAS Geraint: positive: Put 2-14 seconds into all GC rivals on final kilometer gravel, contradicting narrative he arrived under-cooked after injury.
rider_signals.BARGUIL Warren: neutral: French champion attacked on lull but immediately dropped by Landa counter, suggesting form insufficient for GC ambitions.
rider_signals.VAN GARDEREN Tejay: negative: Struggled on penultimate Col des Chevres despite strong Dauphiné form, early indicator he lacks Tour condition.
rider_signals.ALAPHILIPPE Julian: positive: Attacked final km to defend yellow despite stage profile unsuiting him, though legs tied up on 24% gradient.
rider_signals.CICCONE Giulio: positive: Maintained Giro form, understood bonus seconds importance on Col des Chevres, nearly won stage despite being climber not puncher.
rider_signals.KWIATKOWSKI Michal: negative: Lone Ineos domestique before finale reveals team brought insufficient climbing support for defending champion Thomas.
rider_signals.BARDET Romain: negative: Lost over a minute to Thomas on summit finish, catastrophic start to home Tour GC campaign.
